### Post by herbster on 2007-03-30
I have a 20 gig IDE I have plugged in and it seems to read fine, but after editing my fstab with the bolded line:

```
# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/hdb1 :
UUID=e739b8ab-54a3-444f-9370-8e4a5dc8a1e5 / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/hdb5 :
UUID=332a0310-d839-48c1-8317-c204a64306cc none swap sw 0 0
/dev/hdc /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
/dev/hdd /media/cdrom1 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
/dev/sda1 /media/BGPC\040VISTA ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 0
**/dev/hda1 /media/bgpc2 ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 0**
//192.168.1.102/jaycdrive /media/jaycdrive smbfs credentials=/root/.smbcredentials,dmask=777,fmask=777 0 0
```

this is what I get:

```
bobby@bobby-ubuntu:~$ sudo fdisk -l
Password:

Disk /dev/sda: 320.0 GB, 320072933376 bytes
255 heads, 63 sectors/track, 38913 cylinders
Units = cylinders of 16065 * 512 = 8225280 bytes

   Device Boot      Start         End      Blocks   Id  System
/dev/sda1   *           1       38914   312568832    7  HPFS/NTFS

Disk /dev/hda: 20.5 GB, 20520493056 bytes
240 heads, 63 sectors/track, 2650 cylinders
Units = cylinders of 15120 * 512 = 7741440 bytes

   Device Boot      Start         End      Blocks   Id  System
/dev/hda1   *           1        2650    20033968+   c  W95 FAT32 (LBA)

Disk /dev/hdb: 41.1 GB, 41110142976 bytes
255 heads, 63 sectors/track, 4998 cylinders
Units = cylinders of 16065 * 512 = 8225280 bytes

   Device Boot      Start         End      Blocks   Id  System
/dev/hdb1   *           1        4791    38483676   83  Linux
/dev/hdb2            4792        4998     1662727+   5  Extended
/dev/hdb5            4792        4998     1662696   82  Linux swap / Solaris

```

then trying to mount:

```
bobby@bobby-ubuntu:~$ sudo mount -a
NTFS signature is missing.
Failed to startup volume: Invalid argument
Failed to mount '/dev/hda1': Invalid argument
The device '/dev/hda1' doesn't have a valid NTFS.
Maybe you selected the wrong device? Or the whole disk instead of a
partition (e.g. /dev/hda, not /dev/hda1)? Or the other way around?
bobby@bobby-ubuntu:~$
```

I just rebooted and no go still, either.

Help :(

---

### Post by scxtt on 2007-03-30
that drive isn't formatted NTFS, looks to be  vfat ...

---

### Post by herbster on 2007-03-30
> **scxtt said:**
> that drive isn't formatted NTFS, looks to be  vfat ...

Okay, so what do I do? Can I copy over my data somehow first and then format it to ntfs, or what do I have to do?

---

### Post by Motoxrdude on 2007-03-30
does it work fine when you
```
sudo mount /dev/hda1 /media/(what ever you want to mount it as)
```
?

---

### Post by herbster on 2007-03-30
> **Motoxrdude said:**
> does it work fine when you
```
sudo mount /dev/hda1 /media/(what ever you want to mount it as)
```
?

Yup, just tried that! I really need to copy over about 5gigs of data from it to my SATA if nothing else, but having it mounted would be nice.

---

### Post by scxtt on 2007-03-30
you'll need to remove "ntfs-3g" from **/dev/hda1 /media/bgpc2 ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 0** in /etc/fstab and replace it w/ "vfat" ...

---

### Post by herbster on 2007-03-30
> **scxtt said:**
> you'll need to remove "ntfs-3g" from **/dev/hda1 /media/bgpc2 ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 0** in /etc/fstab and replace it w/ "vfat" ...

Okay, I did that, rebooted and it didn't mount, so I did sudo mount -a and here's what happened:

```
bobby@bobby-ubuntu:~$ sudo mount -a
Password:
mount: wrong fs type, bad option, bad superblock on /dev/hda1,
       missing codepage or other error
       In some cases useful info is found in syslog - try
       dmesg | tail  or so
```

So I did dmesg | tail:

```

bobby@bobby-ubuntu:~$ dmesg | tail
[17179667.532000] Bluetooth: RFCOMM TTY layer initialized

[17179780.068000] Inbound IN=eth0 OUT= MAC=00:15:f2:85:82:f1:00:0b:cd:44:05:fc:08:00 SRC=192.168.1.102 DST=192.168.1.101 LEN=41 TOS=0x00 PREC=0x00 TTL=128 ID=16029 DF PROTO=TCP SPT=445 DPT=34992 WINDOW=65041 RES=0x00 ACK URGP=0

[17179784.072000] Inbound IN=eth0 OUT= MAC=00:15:f2:85:82:f1:00:0b:cd:44:05:fc:08:00 SRC=192.168.1.102 DST=192.168.1.101 LEN=41 TOS=0x00 PREC=0x00 TTL=128 ID=16031 DF PROTO=TCP SPT=445 DPT=34992 WINDOW=65041 RES=0x00 ACK URGP=0 
[17179786.076000] Inbound IN=eth0 OUT= MAC=00:15:f2:85:82:f1:00:0b:cd:44:05:fc:08:00 SRC=192.168.1.102 DST=192.168.1.101 LEN=41 TOS=0x00 PREC=0x00 TTL=128 ID=16032 DF PROTO=TCP SPT=445 DPT=34992 WINDOW=65041 RES=0x00 ACK URGP=0 
[17179788.076000] Inbound IN=eth0 OUT= MAC=00:15:f2:85:82:f1:00:0b:cd:44:05:fc:08:00 SRC=192.168.1.102 DST=192.168.1.101 LEN=41 TOS=0x00 PREC=0x00 TTL=128 ID=16033 DF PROTO=TCP SPT=445 DPT=34992 WINDOW=65041 RES=0x00 ACK URGP=0 
[17180589.836000] smb_add_request: request [f6562e80, mid=1] timed out!
[17180589.872000] SMB connection re-established (-5)
**[17180638.280000] FAT: Unrecognized mount option "locale=en_US.UTF-8" or missing value**
```

I think the last line is the problem ?!

????

---

### Post by scxtt on 2007-03-30
remove the locale entry, can't see vfat needing that ...

i'd try "sudo mount /dev/hda1 /media/bgpc2" ... it should be able to "autodetect" it to a degree ...

i have a /dev/hda1 and i just mount it from the CLI when i need it, no /etc/fstab entry ...

if that doesn't work, something must be wrong w/ the partition table and you may want to fsck it ...

---
